behaviors.description: Missing required key in both config file and environment reports exact dotted key path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on handoff across modules (file load + env overlay + required-key validation + error rendering). Unit tests of each piece can miss path propagation/formatting mismatches. Rejected System: behavior text does not require subprocess/real HTTP/external contract; local in-process wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader with a temp config missing the required key and no matching `TOOL_` env var; assert startup failure message contains the exact dotted key path.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires multi-module wiring; a pure unit test would not provide the same reality signal.

behaviors.description: `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` overrides default `~/.config` base for resolving config file path.
behaviors.justification: Needs integration of environment resolution with path-building/loader module. Unit-only checks can miss precedence/order issues between env and default path logic. Rejected System: no explicit external protocol contract or subprocess requirement; local env + filesystem setup is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing a config file; run loader and assert it reads from that location instead of `~/.config`.

behaviors.description: `TOOL_` environment variable value wins over conflicting YAML file key.
behaviors.justification: This is precedence behavior across two sources (parsed file state + env override merger). Unit tests of merge helpers alone can miss end-to-end mapping/key-normalization issues. Rejected System: no real network/subprocess/external contract checks are required.
behaviors.test_description: Provide config file value and conflicting `TOOL_` env var for the same key; assert resulting effective config uses the env value.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The assertion depends on source-merging orchestration, not a single pure function.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax errors include detected line number in the actionable error.
behaviors.justification: Requires real interaction between YAML parser exception data and CLI error formatting. Unit-mocking parser errors can hide mismatch with actual parser exception fields/line numbering. Rejected System: no subprocess or external API/contract validation required.
behaviors.test_description: Load an invalid YAML file in temp config; assert failure message includes parser-reported line number.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real parser + loader wiring; stronger than unit-only formatting tests.

behaviors.description: If config directory is missing at startup, loader creates directory and writes defaults file before continuing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is filesystem side-effect orchestration (existence check -> mkdir -> write defaults -> continue) across modules. Local real filesystem round-trip in test gives needed signal. Rejected higher levels: no explicit external service contract, network, subprocess, or AI dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a temp home/config root where config dir does not exist; run loader; assert directory and defaults file are created and then loader proceeds successfully.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires module interaction plus local filesystem readback; unit-only tests would risk false confidence.
